seoTitle: Drop-Down Lists in Excel-Like Confluence Tables (2026)
metaDescription: Learn how to use drop-down lists in Excel-like Confluence tables in 2026, setting up status options, custom colors, and locked data validation.
intro: Manually typing the same status tags into a table over and over leads to typos and inconsistent formatting. This guide shows how to set up data-validation drop-down lists inside a Confluence table in 2026.

steps.heading: Insert a table
steps.body: In edit mode, click your insertion point and type `/table` to deploy a fresh multi-row grid.

steps.heading: Select your tracking column
steps.body: Move to your target tracking column and highlight your chosen data cells.

steps.heading: Add the status field
steps.body: Type `/status` or open the data options tab to activate the selection drop-down tool.

steps.heading: Set your options
steps.body: Define your custom choices, such as Backlog, In Progress, and Complete, and assign each a distinct color.

steps.heading: Lock the field
steps.body: Use the validation toggle to restrict manual cell overrides and keep entries uniform.

steps.heading: Publish
steps.body: Review the structured grid and click Publish to go live.

faq.question: Can viewers change a drop-down status without opening edit mode?
faq.answer: Yes, interactive cell dropdowns let people update a status directly on the published page without entering edit mode.

faq.question: Can I assign colors to different drop-down options?
faq.answer: Yes, you can assign distinct visual shading, such as different colors for To Do, In Progress, and Done.

faq.question: Can I prevent people from typing in a free-text value instead of picking an option?
faq.answer: Yes, the validation toggle restricts manual cell overrides so entries stay uniform.

faq.question: Can I combine drop-down tables with a project roadmap?
faq.answer: Yes, combining drop-down cell lists with a broader project roadmap gives a clean, scannable overview.
